case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Prior Authorization Department,

We are submitting a prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for our patient Avery Chen, age 61, with confirmed rheumatoid arthritis.

Patient Information:
- Diagnosis: Rheumatoid arthritis (ICD-10 M06.9), RF and anti-CCP positive
- Disease Duration: 22 months
- Current DAS28 Score: 5.1 (high disease activity)

Treatment History:
- Failed conventional DMARDs: 3 documented trials
- Methotrexate trial: 20 weeks at stable dose

This request meets all payer policy requirements:
✓ Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06)
✓ Disease duration ≥6 months (22 months)
✓ Failed ≥2 conventional DMARDs (3 trials)
✓ DAS28 score ≥3.2 (score: 5.1)
✓ Methotrexate trial ≥12 weeks (20 weeks)

Please approve this prior authorization request. Supporting documentation is attached.

Thank you,
Rheumatology Department
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Request - Adalimumab (Humira) for Avery Chen - Case ID: case-002

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

Re: Prior Authorization Request for adalimumab (Humira)
Patient: Jamie Ortiz, Age 50
Case ID: case-007

We are processing your prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for rheumatoid arthritis. The submission meets most payer policy requirements:

✓ Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9, RF and anti-CCP positive)
✓ Disease duration: 10 months (≥6 months required)
✓ Failed 2 conventional DMARDs (≥2 required)
✓ Methotrexate trial: 14 weeks (≥12 weeks required)

However, we require the following additional information:

✗ DAS28 score: The payer policy requires documentation of a DAS28 score of at least 3.2 to establish active disease severity. Please provide the patient's current DAS28 score.

Once we receive this information, we can complete the review and proceed with the authorization decision.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization case-007: Additional Information Required - DAS28 Score

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Prescribing Provider,

We are processing the prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Quinn Patel (Case ID: case-008).

The request meets most payer criteria for rheumatoid arthritis, however we need clarification on one required field:

- Methotrexate trial duration: The policy requires documentation of at least 12 weeks of methotrexate therapy. The current note states the patient is "currently on methotrexate" but does not specify the duration.

Please provide:
1. The start date of methotrexate therapy, OR
2. Confirmation that the patient has completed at least 12 weeks of methotrexate treatment

Once this information is received, we can proceed with submission to the payer.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Additional Information Needed - Prior Auth Case-008 (Quinn Patel - Humira)

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

We are processing a prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Drew Kim (Case ID: case-009) with rheumatoid arthritis.

The request cannot be completed due to missing documentation. The payer policy requires failure of at least 2 conventional DMARDs, but only 1 DMARD trial (methotrexate, 13 weeks) is clearly documented in the clinical note.

The note mentions 'continuation authorization' which suggests the patient may have previously tried other DMARDs. Please provide:

1. Documentation of all conventional DMARD trials (drug names, durations, doses, and reasons for discontinuation)
2. Clarification on which DMARDs failed prior to initiating Humira

All other criteria are met:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9)
- Disease duration: 8 months
- DAS28 score: 3.8
- Methotrexate trial: 13 weeks

Please submit the additional information at your earliest convenience to complete this authorization.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Case-009: Additional Information Needed for Humira Prior Auth - Drew Kim

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

We are processing the prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Skyler Reed (Case ID: case-010).

The request meets most policy requirements, but we require the following additional information to complete our review:

- Disease duration (in months): The payer policy requires documented disease duration of at least 6 months. Please provide the date of initial rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis or confirm the disease duration.

All other criteria are satisfied:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9, RF and anti-CCP positive)
- Failed 2 conventional DMARDs
- DAS28 score: 4.2 (meets threshold of ≥3.2)

Please provide the missing information at your earliest convenience so we can complete the authorization review.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Additional Information Required for Prior Auth - Case case-010 (Skyler Reed - Humira)

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Rheumatology Team,

Regarding the prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Alex Mercer (Case ID: case-011):

The patient meets most policy criteria (disease duration 7 months, 2 failed DMARDs, DAS28 score 3.5, methotrexate trial 12 weeks). However, we need confirmation of the rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis to complete the review.

Required:
- Confirmed rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis with appropriate ICD-10 code (M05.xx or M06.xx)
- The clinical note states 'inflammatory arthritis suspected' but also mentions 'continuation authorization,' which suggests prior treatment

Please provide:
1. Confirmed RA diagnosis with ICD-10 code
2. Supporting diagnostic documentation (lab results, imaging, clinical criteria met)

Once we receive this information, the case can be processed promptly as all other criteria are satisfied.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth case-011: Additional Information Needed - Alex Mercer Humira Request

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

Re: Prior Authorization Request for Adalimumab (Humira) - Patient Sam Rivera (Case ID: case-012)

This prior authorization request does not meet payer policy criteria and is recommended for denial.

DENIAL REASON:
The patient's DAS28 score of 2.4 does not meet the minimum required threshold of 3.2 for biologic therapy coverage. This score indicates low disease activity and does not support medical necessity for adalimumab under current payer guidelines.

CRITERIA MET:
✓ Confirmed rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9)
✓ Disease duration: 10 months (≥6 months required)
✓ Failed 2 conventional DMARDs (≥2 required)
✓ Methotrexate trial: 14 weeks (≥12 weeks required)

CRITERIA NOT MET:
✗ DAS28 score: 2.4 (minimum 3.2 required)

RECOMMENDATION:
If clinical circumstances warrant biologic therapy despite low DAS28 score, please provide additional clinical documentation explaining the medical necessity. Alternatively, consider resubmitting if disease activity increases and DAS28 score meets the threshold.

Please contact our office if you have questions or wish to discuss this case.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Case-012 - Sam Rivera - Adalimumab

case_results.drafted_email:
Re: Prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Robin Hale (case-013)

The prior authorization request cannot be approved at this time due to incomplete documentation.

Missing Information:
The payer policy requires failure of at least 2 conventional DMARDs before approving adalimumab. Currently, only 1 failed DMARD trial is documented.

Please provide:
1. Documentation of a second failed conventional DMARD trial (drug name, duration, dosage, reason for discontinuation), OR
2. Clinical justification explaining why only one DMARD trial was attempted (e.g., contraindications, intolerances, or other medical necessity)

All other criteria are met:
- RA diagnosis confirmed (ICD-10 M06.9, RF/anti-CCP positive) ✓
- Disease duration 12 months (≥6 months required) ✓
- DAS28 score 4.5 (≥3.2 required) ✓
- Methotrexate trial 16 weeks (≥12 weeks required) ✓

Please submit the additional information so we can reprocess this request.

Thank you
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth case-013: Additional Information Needed for Humira (adalimumab)

case_results.drafted_email:
Case ID: case-014
Patient: Jesse Park, Age 51
Drug: Adalimumab (Humira)
Condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis

DECISION: DENIED

Reason: Patient does not meet minimum disease duration requirement. Policy requires at least 6 months of documented disease duration, but patient currently has only 4 months.

Met Criteria:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9, RF and anti-CCP positive) ✓
- Failed 2 conventional DMARDs ✓
- DAS28 score 4.0 (≥3.2 required) ✓
- Methotrexate trial 14 weeks (≥12 weeks required) ✓

Unmet Criteria:
- Disease duration: 4 months (6 months required) ✗

Recommendation: Resubmit prior authorization request after patient reaches 6 months of documented disease duration (approximately 2 months from now). Continue current therapy in the interim.
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth DENIED - Case-014 - Jesse Park - Adalimumab

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Cameron Lee (Case ID: case-015) has been denied based on failure to meet payer policy criteria.

Reason for Denial:
1. DAS28 score of 2.9 does not meet the minimum required threshold of 3.2 for biologic therapy coverage
2. Methotrexate trial duration of 10 weeks is insufficient (12 weeks required)

The patient's DAS28 score of 2.9 indicates low disease activity, suggesting current therapy may be adequately controlling the condition.

Options:
- Continue methotrexate for an additional 2+ weeks to meet the 12-week requirement and reassess DAS28 score
- If disease activity worsens and DAS28 increases to ≥3.2, resubmit the prior authorization with updated clinical documentation
- Consider appeal with additional clinical justification if you believe biologic therapy is medically necessary despite low disease activity

Please contact our office if you have questions or wish to discuss this case.

Sincerely,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denied - Case-015 - Cameron Lee - Adalimumab

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Finley Shaw (Case ID: case-016) has been denied based on payer policy criteria.

DENIAL REASON:
The patient's DAS28 score of 3.0 does not meet the payer's minimum requirement of 3.2 for biologic therapy coverage.

CRITERIA MET:
- Confirmed rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9) ✓
- Disease duration of 8 months (≥6 months required) ✓
- Failed 2 conventional DMARDs (≥2 required) ✓
- Methotrexate trial of 12 weeks (≥12 weeks required) ✓

CRITERIA NOT MET:
- DAS28 score: 3.0 (minimum 3.2 required) ✗

RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Consider reassessing DAS28 score if clinical presentation suggests active disease
- Document any factors that may have affected the DAS28 calculation
- Resubmit with updated DAS28 score if ≥3.2 on re-evaluation
- Consider appeal if clinical justification supports need despite score

Please contact us if you have questions or wish to discuss this decision.

Sincerely,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Case-016: Adalimumab for Finley Shaw

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient River Stone (Case ID: case-028) has been denied based on payer policy criteria.

Denial Reason:
The patient's most recent A1C is 6.4%, which does not meet the payer's required threshold of at least 7.0% for coverage of semaglutide.

Documented Clinical Information:
- Type 2 diabetes diagnosis: Confirmed (E11.9)
- Diabetes duration: 5 years
- Most recent A1C: 6.4% (below required 7.0%)
- Metformin trial: 8 months at max tolerated dose (meets requirement)
- BMI: 31.0 (documented)

The patient's diabetes appears to be adequately controlled on current therapy (A1C 6.4%). The payer policy requires demonstration of inadequate glycemic control (A1C ≥7.0%) to justify medical necessity for semaglutide.

Next Steps:
If you believe this denial is inappropriate, you may:
1. Submit updated A1C results if the patient's control has worsened
2. Request a peer-to-peer review
3. File an appeal with additional clinical justification

Please contact our office if you have questions.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Semaglutide (Ozempic) - River Stone - Case-028

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

Re: Prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient Sloane Hart (Case ID: case-029)

The prior authorization request cannot be approved at this time due to insufficient metformin trial duration.

POLICY REQUIREMENT NOT MET:
- Metformin trial: Patient has completed 1 month, but policy requires at least 3 months at tolerated dose

CRITERIA MET:
- A1C: 7.8% (meets ≥7.0% requirement)
- BMI: 33.5 (documented)
- Diabetes duration: 9 years (documented)
- Type 2 diabetes diagnosis: confirmed

NEXT STEPS:
Please have the patient continue metformin therapy for at least 2 additional months (total of 3 months minimum), then resubmit the prior authorization request with updated documentation showing the completed trial duration.

If there are contraindications to metformin or intolerance issues that prevent continued use, please provide documentation of these clinical factors for reconsideration.

Please contact our office if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Request-More-Info: Case-029 - Semaglutide (Ozempic) for Sloane Hart

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient Aspen Cole (Case ID: case-030) has been denied based on the payer's coverage criteria.

Reason for Denial:
The patient's most recent A1C is 6.9%, which does not meet the payer's requirement of at least 7.0% for coverage of semaglutide.

Documented Clinical Information:
- Type 2 diabetes duration: 4 years
- Most recent A1C: 6.9%
- Metformin trial: 6 months at max tolerated dose (adequate)
- BMI: 28.0 (documented)

Next Steps:
If the patient's glycemic control worsens and a subsequent A1C meets or exceeds 7.0%, a new prior authorization request may be submitted with updated lab values. Alternatively, if there are extenuating clinical circumstances not reflected in the submitted documentation, please provide additional supporting information for reconsideration.

Please contact our office if you have questions regarding this determination.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Semaglutide (Ozempic) for Aspen Cole - Case-030

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

Re: Prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient Winter Lane (Case ID: case-031)

We have reviewed the prior authorization request for Ozempic for type 2 diabetes management. The request cannot be approved at this time due to insufficient metformin trial duration.

POLICY REQUIREMENT NOT MET:
- Metformin trial: Patient has completed 2 months, but policy requires at least 3 months at tolerated dose

REQUESTED INFORMATION:
Please provide ONE of the following:
1. Documentation of completion of at least 3 months total metformin trial at max tolerated dose, OR
2. Clinical justification for why the full 3-month trial cannot be completed (e.g., intolerance, contraindication, medical urgency requiring earlier escalation)

CRITERIA MET:
- Type 2 diabetes diagnosis: Confirmed (E11.9)
- A1C ≥7.0%: Met (7.1%)
- BMI documented: Met (30.5)

Please submit the requested documentation or justification for reconsideration.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth case-031: Additional Information Needed for Ozempic (semaglutide)

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient Eden Marsh (Case ID: case-032) has been denied by the payer.

Reason for denial: The patient's most recent A1C of 6.8% does not meet the payer's requirement of at least 7.0% for semaglutide coverage.

All other criteria are met:
- Type 2 diabetes diagnosis confirmed (6 years duration)
- Metformin trial: 5 months (exceeds 3-month requirement)
- BMI documented: 32.2

Options to consider:
1. Continue current therapy and retest A1C if clinical deterioration occurs
2. If there is clinical justification for GLP-1 therapy despite A1C <7%, consider submitting a peer-to-peer review or appeal with additional medical necessity documentation
3. Explore alternative diabetes management strategies

Please contact our office if you have questions or wish to discuss appeal options.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Case-032 - Semaglutide for Eden Marsh

Dear Neurology Team,

Regarding prior authorization case-044 for patient Faye Knox (age 40) requesting erenumab (Aimovig) for chronic migraine:

The prior authorization request does not meet payer policy requirements and is recommended for DENIAL.

Reason for Denial:
The patient reports 8 migraine days per month, which does not meet the payer's requirement of at least 15 migraine days per month for chronic migraine coverage of Aimovig.

Criteria Met:
- Chronic migraine diagnosis confirmed (G43.7)
- Failed 2 triptan trials (meets requirement)
- Failed 1 preventive medication trial (meets requirement)

Criteria NOT Met:
- Migraine frequency: 8 days/month (requires ≥15 days/month)

Recommendation:
If the patient's migraine frequency has been higher than documented, please provide updated clinical documentation reflecting at least 15 migraine days per month over the past 3 months. Otherwise, consider alternative treatment options covered under the patient's plan.

Please contact us if you have questions or additional clinical information to submit.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Review Team

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

Re: Prior Authorization Request for erenumab (Aimovig)
Patient: Greta Lowe (Age 48)
Case ID: case-045

DECISION: DENIED

The prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) has been denied based on the following:

REASON FOR DENIAL:
The patient does not meet the payer's required diagnostic criteria. The clinical documentation states the diagnosis as 'episodic migraine without chronic migraine features,' while the payer policy requires a chronic migraine diagnosis for erenumab coverage.

POLICY REQUIREMENTS vs. PATIENT STATUS:
✓ Migraine frequency: Patient has 16 days/month (≥15 required)
✓ Failed triptans: 2 documented failures (≥2 required)
✓ Failed preventive medication: 1 documented failure (≥1 required)
✗ Chronic migraine diagnosis: NOT documented

RECOMMENDATION:
If you believe the patient's clinical presentation meets criteria for chronic migraine (≥15 headache days per month for >3 months, with ≥8 days having migraine features), please submit updated documentation with a chronic migraine diagnosis. Otherwise, alternative treatment options should be considered.

If you have questions or wish to submit additional documentation, please contact our utilization management department.

Sincerely,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Case-045: Erenumab (Aimovig) for Greta Lowe
